Name: Museo del Prado
Address: C/ de Ruiz de Alarcón 23 28014 Madrid
Tips: Spain's national art museum — one of the world's greatest. Highlights: Velázquez Las Meninas, Goya Black Paintings, Bosch Garden of Earthly Delights. Free Mon–Sat 18:00–20:00 and Sun 17:00–19:00. Book timed entry in August.

Name: Museo Reina Sofía
Tips: Home to Picasso's Guernica — Madrid's most important single object. Also major Dalí and Miró works. Stand in front of Guernica for 10 minutes. Closed Tuesdays. Free Saturday afternoons and Sunday mornings.

Name: Museo Thyssen-Bornemisza
Tips: Bridges the gap between the Prado and Reina Sofía — Flemish masters through Impressionists to Pop Art. One of the world's greatest private collections. Less crowded than the Prado. Free Mondays.

Name: Palacio Real
Cost Range: €14 (under 5 free; free Wed/Thu for EU citizens)
Tips: Official Spanish royal residence (state ceremonies only). One of Europe's largest palaces — 3,418 rooms. Royal Armoury finest collection of European armour in the world. Arrive at 10:00 to beat queues. Free Jardines de Sabatini adjacent.
Family: Excellent — armour collection great for kids

Name: Parque del Retiro
Tips: Madrid's great central park — 118 hectares, Estanque lake with rowboats (€5–8), glass Crystal Palace, sculptures, free outdoor concerts. Rent rowboats — one of the essential Madrid experiences. Arrive before 10am for peaceful morning walks.

Name: Plaza Mayor
Tips: Magnificent 17th-century arcaded square. Equestrian statue of Felipe III; nine archways into old city. Casa de la Panadería frescoes on north facade. Go early morning (8am) for peaceful atmosphere — arcade cafés overpriced; buy coffee from side-street bar instead.

Name: Puerta del Sol
Tips: Madrid's geographic and symbolic centre — the 0km marker for all Spanish roads. The Oso y el Madroño (bear and strawberry tree) bronze sculpture is the classic Madrid photo stop.

Name: El Rastro
Address: Calle de la Ribera de Curtidores La Latina 28005 Madrid
Tips: Madrid's legendary Sunday morning street market — over 3,500 stalls. Antiques, second-hand goods, crafts, vinyl records. Go at 9am before impossible crowds. Combine with La Latina tapas afterwards. Pickpocket risk — secure valuables. Sundays 9:00–15:00 only.
Family: Good — treasure hunt for kids; watch bags

Name: Templo de Debod
Tips: Genuine 2nd-century BC Egyptian temple donated to Spain and relocated stone by stone. Spectacular west-facing terrace for sunset views over Casa de Campo and mountains. Arrive 45 minutes before sunset — one of the best views in Madrid.

Name: El Capricho de La Alameda
Address: Paseo de la Alameda de Osuna 28042 Madrid
Tips: One of the most extraordinary gardens in Spain — 18th-century Duchess of Osuna's whimsical retreat. Labyrinth, neoclassical pavilions, romantic lake. Open weekends only. Almost entirely tourist-free.

Name: Matadero Madrid
Tips: Massive 1920s former slaughterhouse converted into sprawling cultural campus — theatre, contemporary art, film, design library, vast outdoor terrace. Genuinely local scene. Friday evenings: outdoor drinks, gallery openings, live events.

Name: Lavapiés
Address: Calle del Ave María Lavapiés 28012 Madrid
Tips: Madrid's most multicultural and artistically vibrant neighbourhood. Flamenco bars, international restaurants, independent theatres, street art. The most genuinely diverse corner of the city. Wander Calle del Ave María.

Name: Mercado de San Miguel
Tips: Stunning early 20th-century iron and glass market — upscale gourmet stalls serving jamón, oysters, pintxos, vermouth, croquetas. Arrive at 10am. Best pre-dinner vermut and tapas in the city centre. Mon–Thu 10:00–24:00; Fri–Sat 10:00–02:00.
